sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: Frank Turner, the (1) ______ (ACCUSE) in a trial that has attracted national attention, was today convicted of murder.
The police (2) ______ (INVESTIGATE) lasted for a year and during the trial over 100 hours of (3) ______ (EVIDENT) were heard.
Turner's (4) ______ (LAW) had all argued that he was not in the area at the time, but could not provide the necessary (5) ______ (PROVE).
Police described Turner as a well-known (6) ______ (THIEF) who was responsible for many (7) ______ (ROB) in the local region.
This is not Turner's first (8) ______ (CONVICT). Seven years ago, he was found guilty of (9) ______ (FORGE) and served three years in prison.
The judge is expected to sentence Turner to a period of (10) ______ (PRISON) later this week.
11 I heard that they're going to bring
items.match: in a law banning smoking in public places.

items.sentence: 12 Police are appealing for members of the public to come
items.match: forward with any information they feel might be useful.

items.sentence: 13 Nobody was convinced when the man claimed the gun had gone
14 The policewoman started to take
items.match: down everything I was saying and I knew I was in serious trouble.

items.sentence: 15 Lots of old people have been taken
items.match: in by this trick and have lost a lot of money.

items.sentence: 16 Peterson was found guilty of holding
items.match: up the bank and was sentenced to five years in prison.

items.sentence: 17 Many people get
items.match: away with serious crimes every day.

items.sentence: 18 The policeman decided to let Shaun
items.match: off this time, but told him that he wouldn't be so lucky next time.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
Police said there hadn't been a crime as bad as this in over ten years.
the
Police described it ______ over ten years.

Some people don't respect the law.
have
Some people ______ the law.

The laws in some countries are so strict that people have very little freedom.
such
Some countries ______ people have very little freedom.

The girl was too young to go to prison.
old
The girl ______ to go to prison.

The witness thought I was the thief, but realised that she was wrong.
for
The witness ______ but realised that she was wrong.

Nobody in the country knows the law as well as Mr Parkhurst.
than
Mr Parkhurst knows the law ______ in the country.

There have been so many robberies lately that people are afraid.
such
There have been ______ robberies lately that people are afraid.

Even though we weren't old enough, we managed to get into the night club.
too
Even though we ______ we managed to get into the night club.

The judge told the jury to consider the man's past life.
account
The judge told the jury to ______ the man's past life.
I had ______ a bad time in prison that I never want to go there again.
One problem is that we don't have ______ officers on the streets.
I can't get a car yet because I'm not ______ to drive.
Some crimes seem to be ______ in this country than in others.
Police blamed the robberies ______ a local gang.
My grandma says there was ______ a lot of crime when she was young that nobody trusted anybody else.
It became ______ as I walked home and I began to get slightly nervous.
The prisoner knew he had ______ a mistake and would regret it forever.
The boy ______ that he had had anything to do with the break-in.
Suddenly, someone shouted, 'Thief!' and the man quickly ______ on a motorbike.
Do you have ______ to take that bicycle?
He said it was an accident, but I know he did it on ______.
Things started to ______ wrong for the robbers when the alarm went off.
The sign says that all shoplifters will be ______.
